violations.text: The door to the outside, located in the kitchen, is not tight-fitting. Outer openings of a food establishment must be protected against the entry of insects and rodents by tight-fitting doors. New Violation. Correct By: 04/16/2026
Food and dry ingredients were observed stored on the floor in the kitchen area. Food must be stored at least 6 inches above the floor. PIC inform. New Violation. Correct By: 04/02/2026

Wood is used in the construction of various storage shelving in the kitchen and walk-in cooler areas. Non-food contact surfaces of equipment that are exposed to splash, spillage, or other food soiling or that require frequent cleaning must be constructed of a nonabsorbent and smooth material. New Violation. Correct By: 04/16/2026

Employees' clothing or other personal belongings were observed stored in the kitchen area. Lockers or other suitable facilities shall be used for the orderly storage of employees' clothing and other possessions. New Violation. Correct By: 04/02/2026
Working containers used for storing chemicals taken from bulk supplies were not marked with the common name of the chemical. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 05/15/2025

The door to the outside, located in kitchen, is not tight-fitting. Outer openings of a food establishment must be protected against the entry of insects and rodents by tight-fitting doors. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 03/25/2025

Staff were observed not wearing hair restraints. Food employees must wear hair restraints such as hats, hair coverings, or nets that are worn to effectively keep their hair from contacting exposed food, clean equipment/utensils, linens, and unwrapped single-service and single-use articles. PIC informed. New Violation. Correct By: 05/15/2025
Basin for handsink in kitchen observed cracked. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 04/12/2025

Employees' clothing or other personal belongings were observed stored in the dry food storage area. Lockers or other suitable facilities shall be used for the orderly storage of employees' clothing and other possessions. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 05/15/2025

Raw quahog meat was observed stored above ready-to-eat foods such as milk in the walk-in refrigerator. Raw animal foods must be separated from raw ready-to-eat food such as vegetables during storage. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 03/12/2025

Proper cooling methods were not used for time/temperature control for safety (TCS) foods. Stuffing and chowder were placed hot in the refrigerator in containers greater than four inches deep. Foods were assessed and foods were separated into smaller containers during time of inspection. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 03/12/2025

The door to the outside, located in kitchen, is not tight-fitting. Outer openings of a food establishment must be protected against the entry of insects and rodents by tight-fitting doors. New Violation. Correct By: 03/25/2025
Food must be stored at least 6 inches above the floor. PIC informed. New Violation. Correct By: 03/12/2025
Basin for handsink in kitchen observed cracked. New Violation. Correct By: 04/12/2025

Various counter frames and shelving in the establishment is contructed of unsealed wood. Non-food contact surfaces of equipment must be smooth durable and easily cleanable. New Violation. Correct By: 03/25/2025
